subject: [PATCH] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Fixing David's email address. Sorry! Full quote below.

On Wed, Mar 11, 2026 at 11:43:58AM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: The reviewer, David, did not provide any technical feedback or comments related to the patch, instead mentioning that they still receive emails sent to an old address due to a mailing list subscription.
reviews.raw_body:
I still catch everything sent to the old address as long as a mailing
list I am subscribed to is CCed :)

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer Usama Arif noted that folio_memcg() is called without holding the RCU read lock, which could lead to a lock ordering violation due to its dependency on obj_cgroup_memcg(), and suggested adding an rcu_read_lock() around this call.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Johannes!

I think we need folio_memcg() to be under rcu_read_lock()? 
folio_memcg() calls obj_cgroup_memcg() which has lockdep_assert_once(rcu_read_lock_held()).

folio_split_queue_lock() wraps split_queue_lock() under rcu_read_lock() so wasnt an issue.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the use of list_lru_lock() in an RCU read-side critical section, explained that interrupts are disabled and thus it's safe to use the plain lock, and suggested adding a VM_WARN_ON(!irqs_disabled()) at the top.
reviews.raw_body:
In this case, the caller has interrupts disabled, which implies an RCU
read-side critical section.

That's also why it's using the plain list_lru_lock(), not the irq
variant. Same for the lruvec lock a few lines down. I followed the
example of none of that being documented, either ;)

How do folks feel about a VM_WARN_ON(!irqs_disabled()) at the top?

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a lockdep warning and proposed two possible solutions: using rcu_read_lock_any_held() or making the existing rcu_read_lock() explicit, asking for feedback on which approach to take.
reviews.raw_body:
Okay, lockdep actually does warn. rcu_read_lock_held() is explicit and
not appeased by implicit RCU sections.

Paul pointed me to rcu_read_lock_any_held(), which we could use in
obj_cgroup_memcg() to suppress the splat and permit implicit RCU.

Or we can make rcu_read_lock() explicit in the above code.

Any preferences for how we want to handle this in MM code?

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change improves handling of cgroups on NUMA systems by avoiding splitting large pages across nodes. The patch also streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and adds calls to create node heads for cgroups when splittable pages are created. Additionally, it introduces helpers to the list_lru API for caller-side locking. The deferred split queue lock is replaced with a more efficient approach using list_lru's locking mechanisms.

reviews.author: Dave Chinner
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Chinner requested the patch be split into multiple patches, citing that the current single patch contains complex changes that are difficult to review individually.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you please split this up into multiple patches (i.e. one logical
change per patch) to make it easier to review?

i.e. just from the list-lru persepective, there's multiple complex
changes in the series - locking API changes, new locking primitives,
internally locked functions exposed to callers allowing external
locking, etc. These need to be looked at individually and in
isolation so we can actually discuss the finer details, and that's
almost impossible to do when they are all smashed into one massive
patch.

-Dave.
-- 
Dave Chinner
dgc@kernel.org

reviews.author: syzbot ci
reviews.summary: The reviewer reported a WARNING in folio_memcg due to a lock ordering violation, where vswap_free() acquires the per-vswap spinlock while holding the folio lock, and requested that the lock be dropped before calling try_to_unmap().

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a need for further revision to address Dave Chinner's feedback and promised to send out a v2 of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
No problem, I'll do that and send out a v2.

The list_lru changes started as only the locking functions, then
things kept creeping in...

Thanks

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change improves handling of cgroups on NUMA systems by avoiding splitting large pages across nodes. The patch also streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and adds calls to create node heads for cgroups when splittable pages are created. Additionally, it introduces helpers to the list_lru API for caller-side locking. The deferred split queue lock is replaced with a more efficient approach using list_lru's locking mechanisms.
